feature: /bober-incident skill + CLI entry — top-level incident-workflow entry point
description: Top-level entry that ties the incident pipeline together. /bober-incident slash command (Claude Code skill) + `bober incident start|status|end|list` CLI subcommands. Routes between bober-diagnoser, bober-deployer, bober-postmortemer based on incident phase. Integrates with Tier 2 careful-flow checkpoints for every destructive action regardless of pipeline.mode.

successCriteria.description: skills/bober.incident/SKILL.md (the /bober-incident slash command) exists with YAML frontmatter, Iron Law / Red Flags / Rationalization-Prevention. Body documents the workflow: start incident → invoke diagnoser → propose actions → invoke deployer (with gates) → verify resolution → invoke postmortemer.

CLI: `bober incident start <symptom> [--severity S1|S2|S3|S4]` creates the incident via createIncident (Sprint 19), spawns diagnoser, returns incidentId. `bober incident status <id>` reads incident.json + timeline.jsonl + diagnoses/*.json and prints current state. `bober incident end <id>` triggers postmortem generation + sets status='resolved' if not already. `bober incident list` calls listIncidents (Sprint 19).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Verify each subcommand. Test end-to-end with fixture.

successCriteria.description: Phase routing: src/incident/orchestrator.ts implements an incident state machine. Phases: investigating → remediating → monitoring → resolved. Transitions are deterministic based on agent outputs (e.g., diagnoser proposes next-actions with risky steps → enter 'remediating'; deployer completes + verifyResolution → enter 'monitoring'; SLO holds for criteria.windowMinutes → enter 'resolved').
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read state machine. Test each transition with mocked agent outputs.

successCriteria.description: Checkpoint integration: every destructive action proposed by the diagnoser, executed by the deployer, or surfaced as a runbook step's risky operation passes through the Sprint 20 gate, which uses the Sprint 14 mechanism resolution. Verified by end-to-end test with mode='autopilot' — risky actions still gate via disk mechanism.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Integration test: run an incident workflow in autopilot mode with a risky proposed action; verify the gate fires.

successCriteria.description: `bober incident status` output includes: phase, severity, duration, latest diagnosis with confidence, pending action proposals, executed actions count (with rolled-back count), unresolved hypotheses, resolution criteria if set.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test: status output for various incident states.

successCriteria.description: Slash command (/bober-incident) when invoked from Claude Code: prompts for symptom if not provided, kicks off the workflow, surfaces phase transitions to the user. Output uses markdown rendering for readability.
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Read the skill. Verify the slash-command flow.

successCriteria.description: Escape hatch: at any phase, the user can invoke `bober incident abort <id> --reason <text>`. Writes abort marker, runs rollback (Sprint 21) for executed-not-rolled-back changes IF confirmed, generates a brief abort report (not full postmortem).
successCriteria.verificationMethod: Test abort flow with and without rollback confirmation.

successCriteria.description: Integration test: complete incident lifecycle on a fixture project. start → diagnose → propose risky action → gate approves → deploy → verify → resolve → postmortem. All artifacts on disk in correct order.

generatorNotes:
This sprint is the integration capstone for Tier 3 — every prior sprint flows through this entry point. The complexity is in the state machine, not new logic.

State machine (incident phases):
```
investigating
  → (diagnoser produces next-actions with at least one 'risky') → remediating
  → (diagnoser produces no further actions, all hypotheses ruled out) → resolved (without remediation)
  → (user aborts) → aborted

remediating
  → (all proposed actions executed, postcondition passed) → monitoring
  → (deployer rollback triggered, escalation hit) → escalated (sub-state of remediating, waiting on human)
  → (user aborts) → aborted

monitoring
  → (verifyResolution passes for criteria.windowMinutes) → resolved
  → (verifyResolution fails) → investigating (back to diagnosis)
  → (user aborts) → aborted

resolved
  → (auto-postmortem completes) → resolved (final state, postmortemPath set)
  → (user re-opens) → investigating

aborted
  → (terminal)
```

Phase routing is deterministic — given the current phase and the latest agent output, the next phase is determined. No human-in-loop required for happy-path autopilot (subject to Tier 2 risky-action gates). Document this in src/incident/orchestrator.ts comments.

`bober incident status` output (worked example):
```
Incident: inc-20260524-500-errors-on (symptom: "500 errors on checkout endpoint")
Phase: monitoring
Severity: S2
Duration: 38m 12s

Latest diagnosis (confidence: high):
  Connection pool exhausted after migration 042 deployed double-wrapped transactions
  (diagnoses/diag-1748016000.json)

Actions executed: 2 (0 rolled back)
  1. Scaled deployment api 3→6 replicas (changelog.jsonl#L1) — postcondition passed
  2. Disabled new_checkout_flow flag (changelog.jsonl#L2) — postcondition passed

Resolution criteria:
  api.checkout.error_rate < 0.001 for 10m via obs__datadog
  Current: 0.0008 (sampled 2m ago) — meets threshold; 8m remaining in window

Next: auto-transition to 'resolved' when criteria sustained.
```

Integration test: this is the most complex test in the spec. Use a fixture with mocked observability MCP, mocked deployer (so it doesn't actually call kubectl), and a deterministic diagnoser response. Verify:
1. createIncident produces artifacts.
2. Diagnoser invoked, diagnosis written.
3. Risky action proposed → checkpoint fires (mocked to auto-approve in test).
4. Deployer executes, changelog updated.
5. verifyResolution called with mocked passing metric → status='monitoring'.
6. After windowMinutes simulated → status='resolved'.
7. Postmortem auto-generated.
8. All artifacts on disk in correct order.
9. Audit log shows all checkpoint outcomes.
evaluatorNotes:
Verify state machine transitions are GUARDED — invalid transitions (e.g., resolved → remediating without an explicit re-open) reject with a clear error. Test: try setIncidentStatus from 'resolved' to 'investigating' without using the documented re-open path; should reject.

Verify the integration test is REAL integration — not a chain of mocks. The orchestrator's state machine, the timeline append helpers, the rollback logic, the verifyResolution call — all must be real code in the test. Only the external systems (kubectl, observability MCPs) get mocked.

Verify checkpoint gate fires for EVERY risky action: a test where the deployer proposes 3 risky actions must see 3 gate invocations. Skipping the gate for batched actions defeats the safety design.

Verify `bober incident abort` rollback flow: test that abort with --confirm-rollback runs the inverses; abort without it just marks the incident aborted (does NOT execute rollbacks silently). Silent rollback is a footgun.

Verify the status command works on incidents in every phase including 'aborted'. Should not crash if some artifacts are missing (e.g., aborted incident has no resolution-evidence directory).

Check the slash command flow: when invoked without arguments, prompt is helpful, not silent. When invoked with a symptom, the flow kicks off immediately. Document the user-visible output format so Sprint 26 (VISION.md) can show examples.
